summary: Joe Reis argues that in today's brutal job market, building a personal brand is essential insurance against sudden job loss, as even excellent internal reputations become worthless when you're unemployed. He shares his journey from local Salt Lake City meetups to global recognition through consistent content creation and authentic relationship-building, emphasizing that obscurity—not lack of skill—is the real enemy.
key_points: Everyone has a personal brand whether they acknowledge it or not—it's simply your public reputation
Most people have excellent reputations inside their company but are unknown outside it, which becomes catastrophic during layoffs
Building a brand happens 'gradually and then all at once' through consistent effort over years
Authenticity matters: refusing to be bought and saying what you want builds credibility that transactional shilling cannot
The medium doesn't matter as much as consistency—whether it's meetups, writing, podcasts, or open source
money_quotes: After years of training, playing the game, and 'doing the right thing,' is this what you signed up for? I sense people are rethinking their decisions and realize they can only depend on themselves.
Most people have excellent reputations inside their company but are rarely unknown outside it. This poses a problem if you suddenly find yourself no longer employed by your organization.
You can shotgun a thousand resumes to many companies who will never get back to you, or you can be invited through the back entrance.
When you can't be bought, you have terrific options and can say what you want and grow your credibility in an honest way.
None of this would've happened for Matt and me if we hadn't persisted at content and brand building for years, toiling in relative obscurity in Salt Lake City, a cultural and physical backwater.
key_insight: Obscurity is your biggest career risk—building a visible personal brand through years of consistent, authentic content creation is the best insurance against the brutal reality that your job can disappear at any moment regardless of your internal reputation.
